# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts";
import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/x/kv/mod.ts";

const kv = await openKv();

const validateInput = (input: string): boolean => {
  const regex = /^[a-zA-Z0-9-_]+$/; // Allow only alphanumeric, hyphen, and underscore
  return regex.test(input);
};

const handler = async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const userId = url.searchParams.get("userId");
  const filterParam = url.searchParams.get("filterParam");

  if (!userId || !filterParam || !validateInput(userId) || !validateInput(filterParam)) {
    return new Response("Invalid input", { status: 400 });
  }

  const key = ['users', userId, filterParam];
  const data = await kv.get(key);

  if (data) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), { status: 200 });
  } else {
    return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 });
  }
};

serve(handler);
